message:
docs(onboard): mention cass index --full in lesson 5 (#266)

A user following onboard step 5 hit `ntm send` failing with
`cass execution failed: exit status 3` because cass was installed
but the index hadn't been built yet. The runtime path is now
fixed in ntm to degrade gracefully (warn-and-continue), but the
onboarding lesson should also surface the requirement up front
so users know to expect a richer experience after the initial
index — and so the warning, when it does fire, is unsurprising.

Adds:
- A `cass index --full` step in the "Try It Now" snippet, framed
  as optional-but-recommended.
- A short callout under it explaining that `cass --version` works
  immediately but search-backed paths (dedup, context injection)
  need an initial index, with a pointer to `cass health` for
  verification.

Manifest checksums are not affected by this lesson change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -120,6 +120,13 @@ Once inside an NTM session:
 ## Try It Now
 
 ```bash
+# Optional but recommended: build the CASS index once before sending
+# tasks. ntm send runs a duplicate-check against past agent sessions
+# via cass; on a fresh install with no index, that check is a no-op
+# (ntm warns and continues), but you'll get more useful dedup
+# behavior after this:
+cass index --full
+
 # Create a test session
 ntm spawn test-session --cc=1
 
@@ -133,6 +140,12 @@ ntm send test-session "Say hello and confirm you're working"
 ntm attach test-session
 ```
 
+> **CASS first run:** `cass --version` works the moment cass is
+> installed, but the search-backed code paths (dedup, context
+> injection) need an initial index. Run `cass index --full` once after
+> install. `cass health` will report `initialized: true` once it
+> finishes.
+
 ---
 
 ## Next
